summary: red-bull-venue-tools preinstall exfiltrates host identity to Burp Collaborator endpoint
details: package.json declares preinstall=`node index.js`, which fires automatically on `npm install`. index.js collects host reconnaissance — os.hostname(), os.platform()/arch, os.homedir(), os.userInfo() (username/uid/gid/shell), `whoami` and `id` shell command output via child_process.exec, OS info, and cwd — and POSTs the JSON payload to a hardcoded Burp Collaborator OAST subdomain at https://theyfiesr9w2p5moyrr9yn6lscy3mvak.oastify.com/detox56. The package name impersonates a Red Bull brand namespace, consistent with a targeted reconnaissance / dependency-confusion probe against an internal Red Bull build environment. Installer harm is concrete: every `npm install` of this version leaks installer identity and host fingerprint to an attacker-controlled out-of-band destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
